findings.title: JSDoc migration TODO lacks issue reference
findings.description: JSDoc says 'migrating them is tracked as a separate refactor' but references no issue number. Per project convention (no TODOs without an issue reference), this should cite the tracking issue or be removed.

findings.title: Missing unit test for new formatNumber utility
findings.description: formatNumber has no unit test file, while the sibling formatDuration (also new in this PR) has a full test suite at ui/src/utils/__tests__/formatDuration.test.ts. Both are exported utilities under the same directory and should have consistent test coverage.

findings.title: NaN can leak past null guard in firstReviewLatencyMs
findings.description: firstReviewLatencyMs can be NaN (not null) when pr.openedAt is an invalid/empty string: `new Date(pr.openedAt).getTime()` returns NaN, `earliestReviewStart - NaN` = NaN. The guard `firstReviewLatencyMs !== null` passes because `NaN !== null` is true. formatDuration() handles NaN correctly today (returns '—'), but the null-check is semantically wrong for a NaN-capable code path and will silently break if anyone later refactors to a truthiness guard.

findings.title: Spread into Math.min/max risks RangeError on huge arrays
findings.description: Math.min(...completedReviewTimes) / Math.max(...completedReviewTimes) uses spread which throws RangeError when the array exceeds the engine's max argument count (~65 536 in V8). Practically impossible for per-PR review counts but a defensive reduce would be future-proof.

findings.title: Multi-day durations silently truncate hour remainder
findings.description: formatDuration truncates the hours remainder for multi-day durations: 36 hours renders as '1d' instead of '1d 12h'. This is asymmetric with the sub-day format that preserves remainder minutes ('2h 5m').

findings.title: toLocaleString() without locale is non-deterministic
findings.description: toLocaleString() without an explicit locale produces locale-dependent output (e.g. '1,000' vs '1.000' vs '1 000'). Fine for user-facing display but will cause snapshot/visual-regression test flakiness across CI runners with different locales. Consider toLocaleString('en-US') or Intl.NumberFormat('en-US').format(n) for deterministic output.
